name: session-feedback
summary: UserPromptSubmit hook that makes the harness GOVERN a turn. Runs the real loop (classify->estimate->route->execute handler->record) before the model sees the prompt. Explicit 'os:' commands that complete at high confidence return decision:block — the model is bypassed and the harness's real result is shown (ENFORCED). Everything else injects the trace + an operating protocol as additionalContext (STEERED). Sole writer of record/signals.jsonl. FAIL-OPEN; never blocks on a failed/timeout check.

admission.note: Candidate. Wired in .claude/settings.json (a wired candidate is still a candidate). NOT admitted.

input.description: A Claude Code UserPromptSubmit payload on stdin.

output.description: One visible trace line printed to the session.

exit: Prints the trace and exits 0 — always (fail-open).
invariants: Fail-open: any error is swallowed; the turn proceeds untouched.
ENFORCED: an explicit 'os:' command that completes at high confidence blocks the model (decision:block) — the real handler ran and the outcome was recorded first.
Never blocks natural language (would eat a real question) or a failed/timeout check (availability over false authority).
STEERED, not compelled: the operating-protocol additionalContext is context the model should follow, not a hard constraint.
Sole writer of record/signals.jsonl (signal-ledger is not wired alongside it).
